questions.question: How did most pre-2024 "any-to-any" systems actually work, and what did it cost them?
options: One transformer trained on all modalities

Pipelines — vision model → text representation → speech model → audio — where each hop loses information, adds latency, and complicates training; GPT-4o's single-model subsecond demo showed the alternative
Rule-based format converters
Separate apps stitched by a UI layer
questions.explanation: MIO's answer: tokenize text, image, speech, and music into one shared vocabulary and train a single causal transformer over interleaved sequences — any modality in, any modality out.

questions.question: How does MIO's shared vocabulary host four modalities without collisions?
options: Each modality gets its own embedding matrix

Disjoint ID ranges in one ~48k vocabulary: text 0-31999, image 32000+ (SEED tokens), speech 36096+ (SpeechTokenizer base), music 40192+ (residual-VQ), plus separator tags — one embedding and one output projection span all of it
Tokens carry a modality bit flag
Hash functions map everything to 16k slots
questions.explanation: Each tokenizer emits integers; the allocator just offsets them into reserved ranges. Separators like <speech> tell downstream decoders which renderer the following tokens belong to.

questions.question: How does SpeechTokenizer's residual-VQ structure speech tokens?
options: One flat codebook of 4096 phonemes

8 hierarchical codebooks over the 16kHz waveform — the first level carries coarse content, later levels add prosody and speaker identity; the transformer predicts layer 0 and a parallel decoder fills in the residual layers
Direct character transcription
Raw PCM samples as integers
questions.explanation: This split is what makes streaming viable: the expensive autoregressive model only handles the base layer (~50ms of audio per token), while residuals decode in parallel.

questions.question: What is MIO's streaming-decode latency story?
options: Generation starts after the full utterance ends

Speech tokens stream in every 50ms and consume incrementally; output tokens stream to a parallel speech decoder with ~50-150ms conversion latency — time-to-first-audio-byte ~300-500ms, approaching GPT-4o's ~250ms
Latency is one minute per response
Audio is rendered only after all text finishes
questions.explanation: Conversational use demands <500ms time-to-first-audio-byte. The causal transformer plus streaming tokenizers on both ends is what makes a single open model conversational.

questions.question: What are the three open any-to-any recipes the lesson names?
options: GPT-4o, Gemini, and Claude
AnyGPT (Feb 2024, the proof of concept), MIO (Sep 2024, the scale-up), and Unified-IO 2 (Dec 2023, the cousin with vision + action grounding)
LLaVA, BLIP-2, and Flamingo
Whisper, CLIP, and Stable Diffusion
questions.explanation: All follow the same pattern — per-modality tokenizers feeding one causal transformer — but differ in scale, modality coverage, and whether action/embodiment is in scope.
